name: La Rierada and Santa Creu d'Olorda Loop
name_original: Cascada de Molins de Rei y Santa Creu d'Olorda - circular gravel por el Parque Natural de Collserola
description: This moderate 20.3-mile circular gravel cycling route in Collserola Natural Park features the La Rierada Waterfall and Santa Creu d'Olorda H
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cornellà De Llobregat are situated on the left bank of the Llobregat River, within the Barcelona metropolitan area. The region features riverbanks, the Llobregat Delta Natural Areas with wetlands and coastal pine groves, and access to the Collserola Natural Park. Landscapes range from urban parks like Parc de Can Mercader to protected agricultural zones and coastal paths. This diverse terrain provides varied cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Cornellà De Llobregat The most popular touring cycling route is La Rierada and Santa Creu d'Olorda Loop, a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route ventures into the Collserola Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Montserrat Monastery – Footbridge over the Llobregat River loop from Sant Ildefons, a difficult 66.9 miles (107.8 km) path. This challenging route offers significant elevation gain towards the Montserrat Monastery. Local touring cyclists also love the Castelldefels Beach – Gavà Beach loop from Sant Ildefons, a 53.9 miles (86.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 5 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Cornellà De Llobregat is defined by the Llobregat River and its delta, the Collserola Natural Park, and coastal routes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides on paved surfaces to more challenging routes with elevation gain. The routes in Cornellà De Llobregat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 1300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cornellà De Llobregat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to cycle through in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: Cornellà De Llobregat offers a diverse range of landscapes for touring cyclists. You can explore paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Llobregat River&lt;/strong&gt; and its protected &lt;strong&gt;Delta Natural Areas&lt;/strong&gt;, which feature wetlands and coastal pine groves. Routes also venture into the more challenging terrain of the &lt;strong&gt;Collserola Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, and there are coastal paths leading to beaches like Castelldefels and Gavà. Additionally, you'll find urban parks such as Parc de Can Mercader and the agricultural landscapes of Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. For an easy ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39553622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí del Sorral – Camí de la Bunyola loop from Cornellà&lt;/a&gt;. This route is approximately 19 km long and takes about 1 hour 30 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to touring cycling in the area.

Q: Can I find routes that include natural features like waterfalls?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970723514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada and Santa Creu d'Olorda Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you into the Collserola Natural Park and features the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls in the broader area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2765506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Grotto in Parc de Torreblanca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38145233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montserrat Monastery – Footbridge over the Llobregat River loop from Sant Ildefons&lt;/a&gt;. This route is approximately 108 km long and features significant elevation gain, leading towards the iconic Montserrat Monastery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cornellà De Llobregat are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.2 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, from river paths to coastal views and natural park trails, and appreciate the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Cornellà De Llobregat are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970723514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada and Santa Creu d'Olorda Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores Collserola Natural Park, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24019656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelldefels Beach – Gavà Beach loop from Cornellà&lt;/a&gt;, offering coastal views.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter various landmarks. Some routes offer views of significant sites like the Montserrat Monastery. In the wider Barcelona area, accessible from Cornellà, you can find iconic attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2866399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sagrada Familia&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3142063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Reial&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/397705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arc de Triomf&lt;/a&gt;. Within Cornellà itself, &lt;strong&gt;Parc de Can Mercader&lt;/strong&gt; offers historical buildings and green spaces.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;11 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; available around Cornellà De Llobregat on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 2 easy, 2 moderate, and 7 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that lead to coastal areas?
A: Yes, you can enjoy touring cycling routes that extend to the coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24019656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelldefels Beach – Gavà Beach loop from Cornellà&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that allows you to experience Mediterranean landscapes and coastal towns.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures, making them ideal for touring. Summer can be warm, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred. Winters are generally mild, allowing for cycling, though it's always wise to check local weather forecasts.

Q: Is public transport accessible for reaching touring cycling routes?
A: Cornellà De Llobregat is well-integrated into the Barcelona metropolitan area's public transport network. This provides good access to various starting points for cycling routes. The city also supports cycling with bike-sharing programs, and services in the broader Barcelona area offer bike rentals, making it convenient to explore the region by bike.
tips: A small pleasant waterfall that is unique within the Natural Park of Collserola, in the municipality of Molins de Rei. It is in a protected area for its great ecological value. It is a popular hiking and cycling destination from Molins de Rei or Sant Cugat.

In this area you have a romantic chapel, a restaurant and a picknick area.
The chapel is only open on Saturdays from 11 to 13 o'clock. The restaurant and picknick area are perfect for a stop when hiking through the Collserola park. The restaurant might seem a bit expensive, but for being in the middle of the park it's okay.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate for touring bicycles. While it features diverse terrain including wide gravel roads and some paved sections, be prepared for significant elevation changes. The climb to Coll de Santa Creu d'Olorda, for example, involves a 417-meter gain over 8.9 km with an average gradient of 4.7%, and some segments within Collserola can be quite steep, reaching up to 19-20%.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'circular gravel' description and the varied terrain within Collserola Natural Park, a gravel bike is ideal. While touring bicycles are specified, be aware that some segments can be rugged, so a bike capable of handling both gravel and potentially rougher paths will provide the best experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Molins de Rei?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts from Molins de Rei. While specific parking areas are not detailed, you'll likely find parking options within the town itself, especially near the starting point of the trail. Consider searching for public parking facilities in Molins de Rei before your visit.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. Highlights include the scenic La Rierada Waterfall (Molins de Rei) and the historic Santa Creu d'Olorda Hermitage, which dates back to 1632. The route also passes by the ruins of Castellciuró, a 10th-century Romanesque castle, and offers natural springs like Font de la Tartana for refreshing stops.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful natural scenery within the Collserola Natural Park, characterized by Mediterranean oak and pine forests. From higher elevations, especially around Santa Creu d'Olorda, you can enjoy panoramic views of Barcelona, the surrounding coastline, the Sant Feliu valley, and the Llobregat delta.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty, significant elevation changes, and potentially rugged gravel sections, this specific route might be challenging for younger children or beginner cyclists. While Collserola Natural Park has family-friendly options, this particular circular gravel route is better suited for cyclists with some experience and good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Collserola Natural Park is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and some paths might be dusty. Winters are usually mild but can be damp. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Dogs are generally allowed in the Parc Natural de la Serra de Collserola, but they must be kept on a leash, especially to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always follow local regulations and be mindful of other cyclists and hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the Parc Natural de la Serra de Collserola?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for cycling on the public trails within the Parc Natural de la Serra de Collserola. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be aware of any temporary restrictions or specific rules for certain areas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. Notably, it covers parts of the Sant Feliu de Llobregat a Molins de Rei (33%), Camí del Llobregat (30%), and Camí de Sant Jaume (l'Hospitalet - Tàrrega) (22%). You'll also find smaller segments overlapping with routes like Ruta Mediterrània and El Camí IT3TR2 El Camí Metropolità: de Montserrat a Barcelona.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, the Santa Creu d'Olorda area is a clearing in the forest, and you might find some local establishments or masías (farmhouses) that offer refreshments. Molins de Rei, being the starting point, will have various cafes and restaurants. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the longer stretches within the park.
